specName: Show only lowercase base letters in the diacritic (marks) survey questions
approach: Fold the marks attachment question's base-letter choices to lowercase-only using the same caseCounterpart primitive the character step uses (shared helper in charNormUtils.ts), and add a pure engine helper expandCaseCounterpartAttachments that reproduces the uppercase attachments from the lowercase answers before buildPlacementWorklist. Derivation-only; no locked-contract change.

decisions.decision: Derive casing per-letter via caseCounterpart (shared helper), not a spec-048 IR facet
decisions.why: Matches the character step exactly (FR-006 single source of truth) and is caseless-safe; the spec-048 casing facet is Draft/unwired and the spec permits this interim path
decisions.rejected: Read a casing facet baked into the IR (not yet landed) or guess per-question

decisions.decision: Produce US2 uppercase attachments via a new pure engine helper expandCaseCounterpartAttachments reusing caseCounterpart
decisions.why: buildPlacementWorklist iterates all bases; expanding live answers just before it preserves coverage and keeps casing in the engine; covers plausible-accepted and newly ticked bases
decisions.rejected: Reuse deriveCaseCounterparts (attested stacks only, cannot carry live answers) or toggle uppercase in the UI onToggle handler

decisions.decision: Compute the capitals-follow-automatically count from the folded lowercase base view
decisions.why: SC-004 pins the count to lowercase bases with an uppercase counterpart; deriveCaseCounterparts.size counts attested stacks and can diverge

plan.summary: Studio-side lowercase fold of marks base choices + engine-side uppercase attachment expansion; derivation-only, no contract change
plan.key_finding: The character step has no cased boolean facet; casing is a per-letter caseCounterpart fold, so the shared source of truth is that helper, extracted from PhaseB

tasks.summary: 12 tasks across 5 phases: shared casing-fold helpers (charNormUtils) + pure engine expansion helper (case-fold.ts) as foundational, then US1 display fold + US2 attachment expansion, each with SC-tied tests
tasks.key_finding: US1 and US2 must ship together — the display fold without the engine attachment expansion would silently drop accented capitals; Phase 2 helpers block both stories
